steps.question: What have you been dealing with?
What have you tried so far?
How long has this been going on?
Where are things at right now?
Do you feel like you can figure this out on your own?

report:
You've been fighting on three fronts for one to three years now, and despite trying supplements, dietary changes, prescriptions, and hours of your own research, nothing has actually held. That's not a coincidence. Low mood, stubborn weight, and poor energy aren't three separate problems. They're three symptoms of the same broken system. When your body can't produce energy efficiently, your thyroid slows down, your stress hormones rise to compensate, and your gut starts to deteriorate under the pressure. A sluggish thyroid makes you gain weight and feel flat. Elevated stress hormones drive inflammation that tanks your mood and makes your body hold onto fat. A compromised gut floods your system with inflammatory signals that suppress the very hormones you need to feel motivated, lean, and alive. Each one makes the others worse, and that's exactly why nothing you've tried has lasted. You get a temporary lift from a supplement or a new diet, but the underlying cycle just pulls you back. What you're feeling every day right now, the heaviness, the frustration, the sense that your body is working against you, that's not a plateau. It's a system stuck in a downward loop, and without addressing the actual machinery underneath, the honest reality is it gets harder to reverse the longer it runs.

You've clearly done more than most people would. You've tried the prescriptions, done the research, experimented with supplements and diets. But the reason none of it has stuck is that you've been forced to address these things one at a time, without a way to see how they connect or how fixing one thing in isolation can quietly make another thing worse. This isn't a knowledge gap you can close with more reading. It's an execution problem that requires someone who can see the full picture, sequence the interventions correctly, and adjust in real time as your body responds. Continuing to piece it together on your own means more months of cycling through partial solutions while the underlying pattern quietly deepens. You need a fundamentally different approach, one that works on the gut, the hormones, the metabolism, and the stress response together, because that's how your body actually operates.

See exactly how we do this below.
